Infra_water_electricity_status: mixed: direct ESPAC infrastructure + literature proxy water/electricity
Synthetic_headcount_basis_note: Milk headcount per kg is based on lifetime FPCM per cow using 6.6 lactations of 305 days each.
Synthetic_feed_mapping_note: pasture total = ESPAC pasture + proxy forage + proxy tree forage; cultivated and natural pasture totals show only the ESPAC-linked split of cattle pasture, while unmatched pasture is the residual cattle pasture share not linked to pcnac or sunac; supplement = direct ESPAC per-kg supplement/proxy-mineral feed when available, otherwise ESPAC supplement + proxy mineral from head-day scaling; common = direct ESPAC per-kg common/proxy-compound feed when available, otherwise ESPAC common + proxy compound from head-day scaling; waste = direct ESPAC per-kg waste feed when available, otherwise ESPAC waste from head-day scaling
Infra_water_electricity_note: infrastructure from ESPAC cattle milking-system field; water/electricity from literature proxy
Infra_water_electricity_citations: FAO small-scale dairy manual - Uses 4 to 6 L water per kg dry matter intake; the worked example for a 300 kg cow producing 10 kg milk/day gives about 66 L/day, adopted here as the milk-row water proxy. (https://www.fao.org/4/t1265e/t1275e01.htm) | FAO Milking, milk collection and transport - Gives wash-water benchmarks for hand, bucket, and pipeline milking plus plate-cooler water loads, supporting a dedicated dairy handling infrastructure proxy. (https://www.fao.org/4/i2433e/i2433e07.pdf) | Uruguay MGAP dairy intermediate-consumption report - Provides Latin American dairy infrastructure energy benchmarks for milking-room electricity and milk cooling; a rounded 1.5 kWh/cow/day proxy was adopted as a moderate regional milking-plus-cooling estimate. (https://www.gub.uy/ministerio-ganaderia-agricultura-pesca/sites/ministerio-ganaderia-agricultura-pesca/files/documentos/publicaciones/informe_consumo_intermedio.pdf)

Infra_water_electricity_status: mixed: direct ESPAC infrastructure + literature proxy water/electricity
Synthetic_headcount_basis_note: Milk headcount per kg is based on lifetime FPCM per cow using 6.6 lactations of 305 days each.
Synthetic_feed_mapping_note: pasture total = ESPAC pasture + proxy forage + proxy tree forage; cultivated and natural pasture totals show only the ESPAC-linked split of cattle pasture, while unmatched pasture is the residual cattle pasture share not linked to pcnac or sunac; supplement = direct ESPAC per-kg supplement/proxy-mineral feed when available, otherwise ESPAC supplement + proxy mineral from head-day scaling; common = direct ESPAC per-kg common/proxy-compound feed when available, otherwise ESPAC common + proxy compound from head-day scaling; waste = direct ESPAC per-kg waste feed when available, otherwise ESPAC waste from head-day scaling
Infra_water_electricity_note: infrastructure from ESPAC cattle milking-system field; water/electricity from literature proxy
Infra_water_electricity_citations: FAO small-scale dairy manual - Lactating-cow water use increases with dry matter intake and milk output; a reduced 60 L/day proxy was retained for lower-intensity dual-purpose milk systems. (https://www.fao.org/4/t1265e/t1275e01.htm) | Uruguay MGAP dairy intermediate-consumption report - Latin American dairy energy benchmarks were scaled down to 0.6 kWh/head/day for lower-intensity dual-purpose milk handling. (https://www.gub.uy/ministerio-ganaderia-agricultura-pesca/sites/ministerio-ganaderia-agricultura-pesca/files/documentos/publicaciones/informe_consumo_intermedio.pdf)
